weather_line: Morning light through the hollow · fog lifting by nine · ducks at rest

stories.headline: The Hollow Gives Up Its Last Secret: What the Flat Stone Is For
stories.byline: By our Highland Correspondent, filing from the base of Hollow Crown Ridge
body: On the final morning of the Hollow Crown Expedition, at precisely ten past eight, the light came through the second arch exactly as it had been waiting to. It struck the flat stone shelf at the centre of the hollow and held there — a clean, still column of gold — for forty-seven seconds. Christine stood in it. Steve watched. Neither spoke.

The shelf, Ministry cartographers confirmed this morning via emergency owl (they have been owling a great deal lately, mostly to apologise), is not geological. A carved channel runs its perimeter — too precise for water, too shallow for anything practical. It is, according to a single annotated entry retrieved from the Prophet's archive by our Founding Editor's portrait at approximately six a.m., 'a place for standing still in on purpose.' The entry is dated 1683 and signed only with a small duck.

Christine and Steve broke camp, packed the Jeep, and took the ridge road home at noon. The Jeep did not hurry. The hollow, when they left it, was already full of light again — as though it had been doing this every morning for three hundred and forty-two years, waiting for someone to finally show up and notice.

stories.headline: Ministry Cartographers Request 'One More Week' to Explain the Arch; Christine Sends Back a Single-Word Reply
body: The Department of Magical Cartography, already embarrassed by the discovery of an unmapped arch and subsequently a second unmapped arch within the same hollow, submitted a formal request Friday evening for 'an extended review period of no fewer than seven working days' before issuing any official acknowledgment that the ridge exists.

Christine's reply arrived this morning. It contained one word. Sources close to the Department describe the atmosphere as 'reflective.' A revised map has been promised by Tuesday. The Department's owl returned to base sitting slightly differently than it left.

stories.headline: Lucy Relinquishes Interim Command; Transition Described as 'Seamless' by the Only Witness Who Was Awake
body: With Christine and Steve returning to the household by early afternoon, Interim Commander Lucy has formally stepped down from the position she held for four days. Her administration produced no official record of any kind, which her office notes is itself a kind of record. Three biscuits are unaccounted for. Lucy has declined to speculate.

Ellie, who had not been informed of either the appointment or the expedition, welcomed Christine home with the energy of someone who believed she had been gone for eleven years. Jersey met them at the door, surveyed the situation, determined the warmest lap was now available again, and considered the matter resolved.

stories.headline: Vault Dispute Erupts in Christine's Absence; Settles Itself Upon Her Return Like a Dog Caught on the Furniture
body: A disagreement between two account-holders over the contested contents of Vault 1,104 — which had been escalating since Wednesday through a chain of increasingly ornate formal complaints — was resolved this morning approximately six minutes after Christine walked through the Gringotts doors.

Neither party has offered an explanation. Both have paid their fees. Head Goblin Ragnok issued a brief internal memo noting that 'certain disputes possess the sense to wait.' A framed version has been sent to Christine's desk. She is expected to find it funny, which is why they sent it.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Relinquished command this afternoon with the composure of someone who has already moved on to the next operation. An inventory of the biscuit tin conducted upon Christine's return shows a number that Lucy describes as 'consistent with normal attrition.'

Ellie: Greeted Christine as though she had returned from a very long quest, which, Ellie maintains, she had. Has since introduced herself to the Jeep, apparently for the first time, despite having ridden in it on fourteen separate occasions.

Jersey: Resumed his post on Christine's lap within forty seconds of her sitting down, setting what may be a personal record. Issued a brief purr-adjacent sound of satisfaction and has not moved since, which he considers both a protest and a welcome.
founding_editor_note: That shelf in the hollow? Hand-cut. You can tell by the channel depth — no machine does it that clean. Someone knew what they were doing, and they built it to last. Good to see it finally found the right person to stand on it. — D.

in_brief.sport: The Hollow Crown Ridge ascent has been quietly logged by the Wizarding Highlands Rambling Society as 'category: Christine' — a new classification, above 'strenuous.'
in_brief.forecast: Warm and unhurried through the evening; the hollow holding its light a little longer than usual, as though it enjoyed the company.

storyline_updates.title: The Hollow Crown Expedition
storyline_updates.summary: The flat stone shelf was confirmed as a purposeful, hand-cut standing place, documented in a 1683 archive entry signed with a small duck. Light struck it at ten past eight exactly. Christine stood in it; Steve watched. They left at noon. Arc closed warmly and completely — the ridge has been found, named, and carried home.

storyline_updates.title: The Goblin Election
storyline_updates.summary: A new arc opens: Head Goblin Ragnok has announced he will stand for re-election as Chief Account-Keeper of Gringotts, his first contested election in thirty years. A rival candidate, Sub-Comptroller Griphook the Younger, has filed candidacy papers and requested an independent character reference. Ragnok has, against all precedent and goblin custom, submitted Christine's name as his. She has not yet been informed.
